name: Artificial Intelligence
description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to the development of computer systems that can perform tasks that typically require human intelligence. In particular, this includes Expert Systems, Theorem Proving, Knowledge Representation, Planning, and Uncertainty in AI.

name: Hardware Architecture
description: Hardware architecture in computer science refers to the design and organization of a computer system's physical components and their interconnections. In particular, this includes systems organization and hardware architecture.

name: Computational Complexity
description: Computational complexity in computer science refers to the study of how the resource requirements (such as time and space) of an algorithm or a problem scale with the input size. In particular, this includes models of computation, complexity classes, structural complexity, complexity tradeoffs, upper and lower bounds.

name: Computational Engineering, Finance, and Science
description: In particular, computational engineering, finance, and science includes applications of computer science to the mathematical modeling of complex systems in the fields of science, engineering, and finance.

name: Computational Geometry
description: Computational geometry in computer science focuses on the study and development of algorithms and methods for solving geometric problems using computational techniques. It encompasses a wide range of topics related to the efficient representation, manipulation, and analysis of geometric objects such as points, lines, polygons, and surfaces.

name: Computation and Language
description: Computation and language focuses on natural language processing.

name: Cryptography and Security
description: Cryptography and security in computer science focuses on cryptography and information security in computer science. In particular, this includes all areas of cryptography and security including authentication, public key cryptosytems, proof-carrying code, etc.

name: Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
description: Computer vision and pattern recognition focuses on the development and application of algorithms and techniques for analyzing and interpreting visual data. In particular, this includes image processing, computer vision, pattern recognition, and scene understanding.

name: Computers and Society
description: Computers and society focuses on the reciprocal relationship between computing technology and societal issues. In particular, this includes impact of computers on society, computer ethics, information technology and public policy, legal aspects of computing, computers and education.

name: Databases
description: Databases in computer science focuses on the study, design, development, implementation, management, and applications of database systems. In particular, this includes database management, datamining, and data processing.

name: Distributed, Parallel, and Cluster Computing
description: Distributed, parallel, and cluster computing in computer science focuses on the design, analysis, and implementation of systems that involve multiple computer nodes working together to solve a computational problem. In particular, this includes fault-tolerance, distributed algorithms, stabilility, parallel computation, and cluster computing.

name: Digital Libraries
description: Digital libraries in computer science focuses on the collection, management, preservation, and dissemination of digital information. In particular, this includes all aspects of the digital library design and document and text creation. In particular, this includes combinatorics, graph theory, and applications of probability.

name: Discrete Mathematics
description: Discrete mathematics in computer science focuses on the study of mathematical structures that are fundamentally discrete rather than continuous.

name: Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Data structures and algorithms in computer science focuses on the design, analysis, and implementation of data structures and algorithms.

name: Emerging Technologies
description: Emerging technologies in computer science focuses on new and innovative technologies that are at the forefront of computer science. In particular, this includes approaches to information processing (computing, communication, sensing) and bio-chemical analysis based on alternatives to silicon CMOS-based technologies, such as nanoscale electronic, photonic, spin-based, superconducting, mechanical, bio-chemical and quantum technologies (this list is not exclusive). Topics of interest include (1) building blocks for emerging technologies, their scalability and adoption in larger systems, including integration with traditional technologies, (2) modeling, design and optimization of novel devices and systems, (3) models of computation, algorithm design and programming for emerging technologies.

name: Formal Languages and Automata Theory
description: Formal languages and automata theory in computer science focuses on automata theory, formal language theory, grammars, and combinatorics on words.

name: General Literature
description: General literature in computer science focuses on introductory material, survey material, predictions of future trends, biographies, and miscellaneous computer-science related material.

name: Graphics
description: Graphics in computer science focuses on all aspects of computer graphics.

name: Computer Science and Game Theory
description: Computer science and game theory focuses on all theoretical and applied aspects at the intersection of computer science and game theory, including work in mechanism design, learning in games (which may overlap with Learning), foundations of agent modeling in games (which may overlap with Multiagent systems), coordination, specification and formal methods for non-cooperative computational environments. The area also deals with applications of game theory to areas such as electronic commerce.

name: Human-Computer Interaction
description: Human-computer interaction focuses on the design, implementation, and evaluation of interactive computing systems for human use and the study of major phenomena surrounding them. In particular, this includes human factors, user interfaces, and collaborative computing.

name: Information Retrieval
description: Information retrieval in computer science focuses on processes and technologies involved in the collection, processing, indexing, searching, retrieving, and organizing of information. In particular, this includes indexing, dictionaries, retrieval, content and analysis.

name: Information Theory
description: Information theory focuses on theoretical and experimental aspects of information theory and coding.

name: Machine Learning
description: Machine learning focuses on the development and study of algorithms and statistical models that enable computers to perform tasks without explicit instructions, relying on patterns and inference instead. In particular, this includes all aspects of machine learning research (supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement learning, bandit problems, and so on) including also robustness, explanation, fairness, and methodology.

name: Logic in Computer Science
description: Logic in computer science focuses on all aspects of logic in computer science, including finite model theory, logics of programs, modal logic, and program verification.

name: Multiagent Systems
description: Multiagent systems in computer science focuses on systems composed of multiple autonomous agents that interact with each other. In particular, this includes multiagent systems, distributed artificial intelligence, intelligent agents, coordinated interactions. and practical applications.

name: Multimedia
description: Multimedia in computer science focuses on processing, analysis, retrieval, and understanding of multimedia data, which includes images, video, audio, and other forms of media content.

name: Mathematical Software
description: Mathematical software in computer science focuses on the development, maintenance, and application of software designed to solve mathematical problems.

name: Numerical Analysis
description: Numerical analysis focuses on numerical algorithms for problems in analysis and algebra, and scientific computation.

name: Neural and Evolutionary Computing
description: Neural and evolutionary computing in computer science focuses on the development, analysis, and application of computational systems inspired by natural processes. In particular, this includes neural networks, connectionism, genetic algorithms, artificial life, and adaptive behavior.

name: Networking and Internet Architecture
description: Networking and internet architecture in computer science focuses on the design, analysis, and implementation of network architectures and protocols. In particular, this includes all aspects of computer communication networks, including network architecture and design, network protocols, and internetwork standards (like TCP/IP). Also includes topics, such as web caching, that are directly relevant to internet architecture and performance.

name: Other Computer Science
description: Other computer science focuses on diverse and interdisciplinary topics within computer science that fall outside the scope of more specific categories.

name: Operating Systems
description: Operating systems in computer science focuses on the design, implementation, analysis, and evaluation of operating systems. This includes topics such as process management, memory management, file systems, I/O and device management, security and protection mechanisms, system architecture for operating systems, virtualization, distributed systems, real-time systems, and the interaction between hardware and software at the system level.

name: Performance
description: Performance in computer science focuses on performance aspects of computing systems. In particular, this includes performance measurement and evaluation, queueing, and simulation.

name: Programming Languages
description: Programming languages in computer science focuses on programming language semantics, language features, programming approaches (such as object-oriented programming, functional programming, logic programming). It also includes material on compilers oriented towards programming languages.

name: Robotics
description: Robotics in computer science focuses on the design, construction, operation, and application of robots. This category encompasses a wide range of topics within robotics, including but not limited to robot kinematics, dynamics, control, perception, machine learning for robotics, human-robot interaction, autonomous systems, robotic systems engineering, and the integration of robots into various environments and applications.

name: Symbolic Computation
description: Symbolic computation in computer science focuses on the manipulation, analysis, and computation of symbolic and algebraic information. It includes subfields such as computer algebra systems, symbolic computation, and algorithms for manipulating algebraic expressions, equations, and other mathematical objects. The focus is on the development and application of computational methods and software tools for solving mathematical problems symbolically, as opposed to numerically.

name: Sound
description: Sound in computer science focuses on the processing, analysis, synthesis, and retrieval of sound signals by computational means. In particular, this includes all aspects of computing with sound, and sound as an information channel. Includes models of sound, analysis and synthesis, audio user interfaces, sonification of data, computer music, and sound signal processing.

name: Software Engineering
description: Software engineering in computer science focuses on the design, development, maintenance, testing, and evaluation of software systems. In particular, this includes design tools, software metrics, testing and debugging, programming environments, etc.

name: Social and Information Networks
description: Social and information networks focuses on the study of social networks and information networks within the field of computer science. In particular, this includes the design, analysis, and modeling of social and information networks, including their applications for on-line information access, communication, and interaction, and their roles as datasets in the exploration of questions in these and other domains, including connections to the social and biological sciences.

name: Systems and Control
description: Systems and control focuses on the mathematical theory, analysis, design, and practical implementations of systems and control. In particular, this includes theoretical and experimental research covering all facets of automatic control systems. The section is focused on methods of control system analysis and design using tools of modeling, simulation and optimization. Specific areas of research include nonlinear, distributed, adaptive, stochastic and robust control in addition to hybrid and discrete event systems. Application areas include automotive and aerospace control systems, network control, biological systems, multiagent and cooperative control, robotics, reinforcement learning, sensor networks, control of cyber-physical and energy-related systems, and control of computing systems.

name: Econometrics
description: Econometrics focuses on using statistical methods to economic data to give empirical content to economic relationships. It involves the use of mathematical models to test hypotheses and forecast future trends in economic activity. In particular, this includes Econometric Theory, Micro-Econometrics, Macro-Econometrics, Empirical Content of Economic Relations discovered via New Methods, and Methodological Aspects of the Application of Statistical Inference to Economic Data.

name: General Economics
description: General economics focuses on general methodological, applied, and empirical contributions to economics, including micro and macro economics, international economics, theory of the firm, labor economics, and other economic topics outside finance.

name: Theoretical Economics
description: Theoretical economics focuses on the theoretical aspects of economics. In particular, this includes theoretical contributions to Contract Theory, Decision Theory, Game Theory, General Equilibrium, Growth, Learning and Evolution, Macroeconomics, Market and Mechanism Design, and Social Choice.

name: Audio and Speech Processing
description: Audio and speech processing focuses on theory and methods for processing signals representing audio, speech, and language, and their applications. This includes analysis, synthesis, enhancement, transformation, classification and interpretation of such signals as well as the design, development, and evaluation of associated signal processing systems. Machine learning and pattern analysis applied to any of the above areas is also welcome. Specific topics of interest include: auditory modeling and hearing aids; acoustic beamforming and source localization; classification of acoustic scenes; speaker separation; active noise control and echo cancellation; enhancement; de-reverberation; bioacoustics; music signals analysis, synthesis and modification; music information retrieval; audio for multimedia and joint audio-video processing; spoken and written language modeling, segmentation, tagging, parsing, understanding, and translation; text mining; speech production, perception, and psychoacoustics; speech analysis, synthesis, and perceptual modeling and coding; robust speech recognition; speaker recognition and characterization; deep learning, online learning, and graphical models applied to speech, audio, and language signals; and implementation aspects ranging from system architecture to fast algorithms.

name: Image and Video Processing
description: Image and video processing focuses on theory, algorithms, and architectures for the formation, capture, processing, communication, analysis, and display of images, video, and multidimensional signals in a wide variety of applications. Topics of interest include: mathematical, statistical, and perceptual image and video modeling and representation; linear and nonlinear filtering, de-blurring, enhancement, restoration, and reconstruction from degraded, low-resolution or tomographic data; lossless and lossy compression and coding; segmentation, alignment, and recognition; image rendering, visualization, and printing; computational imaging, including ultrasound, tomographic and magnetic resonance imaging; and image and video analysis, synthesis, storage, search and retrieval.

name: Signal Processing
description: Signal processing focuses on theory, algorithms, performance analysis and applications of signal and data analysis, including physical modeling, processing, detection and parameter estimation, learning, mining, retrieval, and information extraction. The term "signal" includes speech, audio, sonar, radar, geophysical, physiological, (bio-) medical, image, video, and multimodal natural and man-made signals, including communication signals and data. Topics of interest include: statistical signal processing, spectral estimation and system identification; filter design, adaptive filtering / stochastic learning; (compressive) sampling, sensing, and transform-domain methods including fast algorithms; signal processing for machine learning and machine learning for signal processing applications; in-network and graph signal processing; convex and nonconvex optimization methods for signal processing applications; radar, sonar, and sensor array beamforming and direction finding; communications signal processing; low power, multi-core and system-on-chip signal processing; sensing, communication, analysis and optimization for cyber-physical systems such as power grids and the Internet of Things.

name: Systems and Control
description: Systems and control focuses on the mathematical theory, analysis, design, and practical implementations of systems and control. In particular, this includes theoretical and experimental research covering all facets of automatic control systems. The section is focused on methods of control system analysis and design using tools of modeling, simulation and optimization. Specific areas of research include nonlinear, distributed, adaptive, stochastic and robust control in addition to hybrid and discrete event systems. Application areas include automotive and aerospace control systems, network control, biological systems, multiagent and cooperative control, robotics, reinforcement learning, sensor networks, control of cyber-physical and energy-related systems, and control of computing systems.

name: Commutative Algebra
description: Commutative algebra in mathematics focuses on the study of commutative rings, modules, ideals, homological algebra, computational aspects, invariant theory, connections to algebraic geometry and combinatorics

name: Algebraic Geometry
description: Algebraic geometry in mathematics focuses on  geometric properties and structures that are defined algebraically using polynomial equations. In particular, this includes algebraic varieties, stacks, sheaves, schemes, moduli spaces, complex geometry, and quantum cohomology.

name: Analysis of PDEs
description: Analysis of PDEs in mathematics focuses on partial differential equations (PDEs). In particular, this includes existence and uniqueness, boundary conditions, linear and non-linear operators, stability, soliton theory, integrable PDE's, and conservation laws, qualitative dynamics

name: Algebraic Topology
description: Algebraic topology in mathematics focuses on homotopy theory, homological algebra, and algebraic treatments of manifolds.

name: Classical Analysis and ODEs
description: Classical analysis and ODEs in mathematics focuses on classical analysis and the theory and application of ordinary differential equations. In particular, this includes special functions, orthogonal polynomials, harmonic analysis, ODE's, differential relations, calculus of variations, approximations, expansions, asymptotics.

name: Combinatorics
description: Combinatorics in mathematics focuses on the study of finite or countable discrete structures. In particular, this includes discrete mathematics, graph theory, enumeration, combinatorial optimization, Ramsey theory, and combinatorial game theory.

name: Category Theory
description: Category theory in mathematics focuses on the abstract study of structures and systems using categories. In particular, this involves enriched categories, topoi, abelian categories, monoidal categories, and homological algebra.

name: Complex Variables
description: Complex variables in mathematics focuses on the study of functions of complex numbers, complex manifolds, analytic spaces, and related subjects. In particuler, this includes holomorphic functions, automorphic group actions and forms, pseudoconvexity, complex geometry, analytic spaces, and analytic sheaves.

name: Differential Geometry
description: Differential geometry in mathematics focuses on the techniques of differential and integral calculus, as well as linear and multilinear algebra, to study problems involving curves, surfaces, and more generally, geometric structures on differentiable manifolds. In particular, this includes complex, contact, Riemannian, pseudo-Riemannian and Finsler geometry, relativity, gauge theory, and global analysis.

name: Dynamical Systems
description: Dynamical systems in mathematics focuses on the study of systems that evolve over time according to a set of rules or equations. In particular, this involves dynamics of differential equations and flows, mechanics, classical few-body problems, iterations, complex dynamics, delayed differential equations.

name: Functional Analysis
description: Functional analysis in mathematics focuses on the study of vector spaces endowed with some kind of limit-related structure (e.g., inner product, norm, topology) and the linear operators acting upon these spaces. In particular, this includes banach spaces, function spaces, real functions, integral transforms, theory of distributions, and measure theory.

name: General Mathematics
description: General mathematics focuses on mathematical material of general interest with topics that fall outside the scope of more specific categories.

name: General Topology
description: General topology in mathematics focuses on the basic set-theoretic definitions and constructions used in topology. In particular, this includes continuum theory, point-set topology, spaces with algebraic structure, foundations, dimension theory, local and global properties.

name: Group Theory
description: Group theory in mathematics focuses on the study of groups, which are algebraic structures consisting of a set of elements equipped with a single binary operation that combines any two elements to form a third element, in such a way that the operation satisfies four fundamental properties: closure, associativity, the existence of an identity element, and the existence of inverse elements for each element in the group. In particular, this includes finite groups, topological groups, representation theory, cohomology, classification and structure.

name: Geometric Topology
description: Geometric topology in mathematics focuses on the study of geometric and topological properties of spaces and manifolds. In particular, this includes manifolds, orbifolds, polyhedra, cell complexes, foliations, and geometric structures.

name: History and Overview
description: History and overview in mathematics focuses on the historical aspects and broad survey articles in mathematics. In particular, this includes biographies, philosophy of mathematics, mathematics education, recreational mathematics, communication of mathematics, and ethics in mathematics.

name: Information Theory
description: Information theory focuses on theoretical and experimental aspects of information theory and coding.

name: K-Theory and Homology
description: K-theory is an area of algebraic topology that deals with the study of vector bundles and their generalizations; it provides a framework for understanding various topological and algebraic invariants of spaces and rings. Homology, on the other hand, is a fundamental concept in algebraic topology that involves the construction of algebraic objects (homology groups) to probe topological spaces, allowing for the classification and examination of their properties. In particular, this includes algebraic and topological K-theory, relations with topology, commutative algebra, and operator algebras.

name: Logic
description: Logic in mathematics focuses on mathematical logic and foundations. In particular, this includes logic, set theory, point-set topology, and formal mathematics.

name: Metric Geometry
description: Metric geometry in mathematics focuses on the study of geometric properties and structures that are defined in terms of distances. In particular, this includes euclidean, hyperbolic, discrete, convex, coarse geometry, comparisons in Riemannian geometry, and symmetric spaces.

name: Mathematical Physics
description: Mathematical physics focuses on areas of research that illustrate the application of mathematics to problems in physics, develop mathematical methods for such applications, or provide mathematically rigorous formulations of existing physical theories.

name: Numerical Analysis
description: Numerical analysis focuses on numerical algorithms for problems in analysis and algebra, and scientific computation.

name: Number Theory
description: Number theory in mathematics focuses on the properties and relationships of numbers, particularly integers. In particular, this includes prime numbers, diophantine equations, analytic number theory, algebraic number theory, arithmetic geometry, and Galois theory.

name: Operator Algebras
description: Operator algebras in mathematics focuses on linear operators on function spaces and the algebraic structures that arise from them. In particular, this includes algebras of operators on Hilbert space, C^*-algebras, von Neumann algebras, and non-commutative geometry.

name: Optimization and Control
description: Optimization and control in mathematics focuses on operations research, linear programming, control theory, systems theory, optimal control, and game theory.

name: Probability
description: Probability in mathematics focuses on theory and applications of probability and stochastic processes: e.g. central limit theorems, large deviations, stochastic differential equations, models from statistical mechanics, and queuing theory.

name: Quantum Algebra
description: Quantum algebra in mathematics focuses on algebraic structures motivated by quantum theory. In particular, this includes quantum groups, skein theories, operadic and diagrammatic algebra, and quantum field theory.

name: Rings and Algebras
description: Rings and algebras in mathematics focuses on the study of rings, algebras, and related structures. In particular, this includes non-commutative rings and algebras, non-associative algebras, universal algebra and lattice theory, linear algebra, and semigroups.

name: Representation Theory
description: Representation theory in mathematics focuses on abstract algebraic structures by representing their elements as linear transformations of vector spaces, and by studying modules over these structures. In particular, this includes linear representations of algebras and groups, Lie theory, associative algebras, and multilinear algebra.

name: Symplectic Geometry
description: Symplectic geometry in mathematics focuses on symplectic manifolds, which are smooth manifolds equipped with a closed, nondegenerate 2-form called a symplectic form. In particular, this includes hamiltonian systems, symplectic flows, and classical integrable systems.

name: Spectral Theory
description: Spectral theory in mathematics focuses on the study of spectral properties of linear operators, particularly those defined on infinite-dimensional spaces such as Hilbert spaces. In particular, this includes Schrodinger operators, operators on manifolds, general differential operators, numerical studies, integral operators, discrete models, resonances, non-self-adjoint operators, and random operators/matrices.

name: Statistics Theory
description: Statistics theory in mathematics focuses on applied, computational and theoretical statistics. In particular, this includes statistical inference, regression, time series, multivariate analysis, data analysis, Markov chain Monte Carlo, design of experiments, and case studies. It also includes Asymptotics, Bayesian Inference, Decision Theory, Estimation, Foundations, Inference, Testing.

name: Astrophysics
description: Astrophysics is a science that employs the methods and principles of physics and chemistry in the study of astronomical objects and phenomena.

name: Cosmology and Nongalactic Astrophysics
description: Cosmology and nongalactic astrophysics focuses on phenomenology of early universe, cosmic microwave background, cosmological parameters, primordial element abundances, extragalactic distance scale, and large-scale structure of the universe. Groups, superclusters, voids, intergalactic medium. Particle astrophysics: dark energy, dark matter, baryogenesis, leptogenesis, inflationary models, reheating, monopoles, WIMPs, cosmic strings, primordial black holes, and cosmological gravitational radiation.

name: Earth and Planetary Astrophysics
description: Earth and planetary astrophysics focuses on interplanetary medium, planetary physics, planetary astrobiology, extrasolar planets, comets, asteroids, and meteorites. In addition, it includes structure and formation of the solar system.

name: Astrophysics of Galaxies
description: Astrophysics of galaxies focuses on phenomena pertaining to galaxies or the Milky Way. Star clusters, HII regions and planetary nebulae, the interstellar medium, atomic and molecular clouds, dust. Stellar populations. Galactic structure, formation, dynamics. Galactic nuclei, bulges, disks, halo. Active Galactic Nuclei, supermassive black holes, quasars. Gravitational lens systems. The Milky Way and its contents.

name: High Energy Astrophysical Phenomena
description: High energy astrophysical phenomena focuses on cosmic ray production, acceleration, propagation, detection. Gamma ray astronomy and bursts, X-rays, charged particles, supernovae and other explosive phenomena, stellar remnants and accretion systems, jets, microquasars, neutron stars, pulsars, and black holes.

name: Instrumentation and Methods for Astrophysics
description: Instrumentation and methods for astrophysics focuses on detector and telescope design, experiment proposals. Laboratory Astrophysics. Methods for data analysis, statistical methods. Software, database design.

name: Solar and Stellar Astrophysics
description: Solar and stellar astrophysics focuses on white dwarfs, brown dwarfs, cataclysmic variables. Star formation and protostellar systems, stellar astrobiology, binary and multiple systems of stars, stellar evolution and structure, coronas. Central stars of planetary nebulae. Helioseismology, solar neutrinos, production and detection of gravitational radiation from stellar systems.

name: Condensed Matter
description: Condensed matter physics is the field of physics that deals with the macroscopic and microscopic physical properties of matter, especially the solid and liquid phases that arise from electromagnetic forces between atoms and electrons.

name: Disordered Systems and Neural Networks
description: Disordered systems and neural networks in condensed matter physics focuses on physical systems that are inherently non-crystalline or lack long-range order. In particular, this includes glasses and spin glasses, properties of random, aperiodic and quasiperiodic systems, transport in disordered media, localization, phenomena mediated by defects and disorder, and neural networks.

name: Mesoscale and Nanoscale Physics
description: Mesoscale and nanoscale physics in condensed matter physics focuses on the physical properties and behaviors of systems with dimensions at the mesoscopic scale (intermediate between microscopic and macroscopic scales) and the nanoscale (typically less than 100 nanometers). In particular, this includes semiconducting nanostructures: quantum dots, wires, and wells. Single electronics, spintronics, 2d electron gases, quantum Hall effect, nanotubes, graphene, and plasmonic nanostructures.

name: Materials Science
description: Materials science in condensed matter physics focuses on the properties and behaviors of materials from a condensed matter perspective. In particular, this includes techniques, synthesis, characterization, structure. Structural phase transitions, mechanical properties, phonons. Defects, adsorbates, interfaces.

name: Other Condensed Matter
description: Other condensed matter in physics focuses on topics that fall outside the scope of more specific categories.

name: Quantum Gases
description: Quantum gases in condensed matter physics focuses on ultracold atomic and molecular gases, Bose-Einstein condensation, Feshbach resonances, spinor condensates, optical lattices, quantum simulation with cold atoms and molecules, and macroscopic interference phenomena.

name: Soft Condensed Matter
description: Soft condensed matter in physics focuses on the study of materials that are easily deformable by external fields or internal stresses. In particular, this includes membranes, polymers, liquid crystals, glasses, colloids, and granular matter.

name: Statistical Mechanics
description: Statistical mechanics in condensed matter physics focuses on statistical properties of systems with a large number of particles and the laws that govern their behavior. In particular, this includes phase transitions, thermodynamics, field theory, non-equilibrium phenomena, renormalization group and scaling, integrable models, and turbulence.

name: Strongly Correlated Electrons
description: Strongly correlated electrons in condensed matter physics focuses on systems where interactions between electrons are so strong that they cannot be described adequately by non-interacting models. In particular, this includes quantum magnetism, non-Fermi liquids, spin liquids, quantum criticality, charge density waves, and metal-insulator transitions.

name: Superconductivity
description: Superconductivity in condensed matter physics focuses on the study of materials and phenomena where electrical resistance drops to zero below a certain critical temperature, allowing for the flow of electric current without energy loss. In particular, this includes superconductivity: theory, models, experiment. Superflow in helium.

name: General Relativity and Quantum Cosmology
description: General relativity and quantum cosmology focuses on gravitational physics, including experiments and observations related to the detection and interpretation of gravitational waves, experimental tests of gravitational theories, computational general relativity, relativistic astrophysics, solutions to Einstein's equations and their properties, alternative theories of gravity, classical and quantum cosmology, and quantum gravity.

name: High Energy Physics - Experiment
description: High energy physics - experiment focuses on experimental research in high energy physics. This includes the design, construction, and results of experiments that probe the fundamental particles of matter and the forces that govern their interactions. Research in this category typically involves studies conducted at large-scale particle accelerators, such as the Large Hadron Collider (LHC), and other experimental facilities that investigate phenomena like the Higgs boson, quarks, leptons, and beyond the Standard Model physics.

name: High Energy Physics - Lattice
description: High energy physics - lattice focuses on lattice field theory as a method to investigate the properties of quantum fields and strongly interacting systems, such as quantum chromodynamics (QCD), which is the theory of the strong interaction between quarks and gluons. Lattice field theory provides a non-perturbative approach to studying particle physics, allowing for numerical simulations of theories that are difficult or impossible to solve analytically. In particular, this includes lattice field theory. Phenomenology from lattice field theory. Algorithms for lattice field theory. Hardware for lattice field theory.

name: High Energy Physics - Phenomenology
description: High energy physics - phenomenology focuses on theoretical and phenomenological research related to the understanding of fundamental particles and forces at high energies. In particular, this includes theoretical particle physics and its interrelation with experiment. Prediction of particle physics observables: models, effective field theories, calculation techniques. Particle physics: analysis of theory through experimental results.

name: High Energy Physics - Theory
description: High energy physics - theory focuses on formal aspects of quantum field theory. String theory, supersymmetry and supergravity.

name: Mathematical Physics
description: Mathematical physics focuses on areas of research that illustrate the application of mathematics to problems in physics, develop mathematical methods for such applications, or provide mathematically rigorous formulations of existing physical theories.

name: Adaptation and Self-Organizing Systems
description: Adaptation and self-organizing systems in nonlinear sciences physics focuses on systems that exhibit adaptive behavior and self-organization. In particular, this includes adaptation, self-organizing systems, statistical physics, fluctuating systems, stochastic processes, interacting particle systems, and machine learning.

name: Chaotic Dynamics
description: Chaotic dynamics in nonlinear sciences physics focuses on the study of chaotic systems and dynamical systems that exhibit sensitive dependence on initial conditions, leading to behavior that appears to be random or unpredictable even though the systems are deterministic in nature. in paticular, this includes dynamical systems, chaos, quantum chaos, topological dynamics, cycle expansions, turbulence, and propagation.

name: Cellular Automata and Lattice Gases
description: Cellular automata and lattice gases in nonlinear sciences physics focuses on the study of cellular automata (CA) and lattice gas models, which are discrete, spatially extended dynamical systems used to model complex behavior in a variety of scientific and mathematical contexts. In particular, this includes computational methods, time series analysis, signal processing, wavelets, and lattice gases.

name: Pattern Formation and Solitons
description: Pattern formation and solitons in nonlinear sciences physics focuses on the study of spontaneous pattern formation in physical, biological, chemical, and related systems. In particular, this includes pattern formation, coherent structures, and solitons.

name: Exactly Solvable and Integrable Systems
description: Exactly solvable and integrable systems in nonlinear sciences physics focuses on systems that can be solved exactly through analytical methods or have properties that allow for integrability. In particular, this includes exactly solvable systems, integrable PDEs, integrable ODEs, Painleve analysis, integrable discrete maps, solvable lattice models, and integrable quantum systems.

name: Nuclear Experiment
description: Nuclear experiment in physics focuses on the areas of fundamental interactions, measurements at low- and medium-energy, as well as relativistic heavy-ion collisions. Does not include: detectors and instrumentation nor analysis methods to conduct experiments; descriptions of experimental programs (present or future); comments on published results.

name: Nuclear Theory
description: Nuclear theory in physics focuses on theory of nuclear structure covering wide area from models of hadron structure to neutron stars. Nuclear equation of states at different external conditions. Theory of nuclear reactions including heavy-ion reactions at low and high energies. It does not include problems of data analysis, physics of nuclear reactors, problems of safety, reactor construction.

name: Accelerator Physics
description: Accelerator physics focuses on accelerator theory and simulation. Accelerator technology. Accelerator experiments. Beam Physics. Accelerator design and optimization. Advanced accelerator concepts. Radiation sources including synchrotron light sources and free electron lasers. Applications of accelerators.

name: Atmospheric and Oceanic Physics
description: Atmospheric and oceanic physics focuses on the physical processes occurring in the Earth's atmosphere and oceans. In particular, this includes atmospheric and oceanic physics and physical chemistry, biogeophysics, and climate science.

name: Applied Physics
description: Applied physics focuses on applications of physics to new technology, including electronic devices, optics, photonics, microwaves, spintronics, advanced materials, metamaterials, nanotechnology, and energy sciences.

name: Atomic and Molecular Clusters
description: Atomic and molecular clusters in physics focuses on atomic and molecular clusters, nanoparticles: geometric, electronic, optical, chemical, magnetic properties, shell structure, phase transitions, optical spectroscopy, mass spectrometry, photoelectron spectroscopy, ionization potential, electron affinity, interaction with intense light pulses, electron diffraction, light scattering, ab initio calculations, DFT theory, fragmentation, Coulomb explosion, hydrodynamic expansion.

name: Atomic Physics
description: Atomic physics focuses on atomic and molecular structure, spectra, collisions, and data. Atoms and molecules in external fields. Molecular dynamics and coherent and optical control. Cold atoms and molecules. Cold collisions. Optical lattices.

name: Biological Physics
description: Biological physics focuses on molecular biophysics, cellular biophysics, neurological biophysics, membrane biophysics, single-molecule biophysics, ecological biophysics, quantum phenomena in biological systems (quantum biophysics), theoretical biophysics, molecular dynamics/modeling and simulation, game theory, biomechanics, bioinformatics, microorganisms, virology, evolution, biophysical methods.

name: Chemical Physics
description: Chemical physics focuses on experimental, computational, and theoretical physics of atoms, molecules, and clusters - Classical and quantum description of states, processes, and dynamics; spectroscopy, electronic structure, conformations, reactions, interactions, and phases. Chemical thermodynamics. Disperse systems. High pressure chemistry. Solid state chemistry. Surface and interface chemistry.

name: Classical Physics
description: Classical physics focuses on newtonian and relativistic dynamics; many particle systems; planetary motions; chaos in classical dynamics. Maxwell's equations and dynamics of charged systems and electromagnetic forces in materials. Vibrating systems such as membranes and cantilevers; optomechanics. Classical waves, including acoustics and elasticity; physics of music and musical instruments. Classical thermodynamics and heat flow problems.

name: Computational Physics
description: Computational physics focuses on all aspects of computational science applied to physics.

name: Data Analysis, Statistics and Probability
description: Data analysis, statistics and probability in physics focuses on methods, software and hardware for physics data analysis: data processing and storage; measurement methodology; statistical and mathematical aspects such as parametrization and uncertainties.

name: Physics Education
description: Physics education focuses on report of results of a research study, laboratory experience, assessment or classroom practice that represents a way to improve teaching and learning in physics. Also, report on misconceptions of students, textbook errors, and other similar information relative to promoting physics understanding.

name: Fluid Dynamics
description: Fluid dynamics in physics focuses on turbulence, instabilities, incompressible/compressible flows, reacting flows. Aero/hydrodynamics, fluid-structure interactions, acoustics. Biological fluid dynamics, micro/nanofluidics, interfacial phenomena. Complex fluids, suspensions and granular flows, porous media flows. Geophysical flows, thermoconvective and stratified flows. Mathematical and computational methods for fluid dynamics, fluid flow models, experimental techniques.

name: General Physics
description: General physics focuses on multidisciplinary topics, speculative ideas, and proposals that cover more than one area of physics. It may also include foundational issues, philosophical aspects of physics, or novel theoretical frameworks that are not yet widely recognized or do not fall under the traditional disciplines.

name: Geophysics
description: Geophysics focuses on atmospheric physics. Biogeosciences. Computational geophysics. Geographic location. Geoinformatics. Geophysical techniques. Hydrospheric geophysics. Magnetospheric physics. Mathematical geophysics. Planetology. Solar system. Solid earth geophysics. Space plasma physics. Mineral physics. High pressure physics.

name: History and Philosophy of Physics
description: History and philosophy of physics focuses on history and philosophy of all branches of physics, astrophysics, and cosmology, including appreciations of physicists.

name: Instrumentation and Detectors
description: Instrumentation and detectors in physics focuses on instrumentation and detectors for research in natural science, including optical, molecular, atomic, nuclear and particle physics instrumentation and the associated electronics, services, infrastructure and control equipment.

name: Medical Physics
description: Medical physics focuses on radiation therapy. Radiation dosimetry. Biomedical imaging modelling. Reconstruction, processing, and analysis. Biomedical system modelling and analysis. Health physics. New imaging or therapy modalities.

name: Optics
description: Optics in physics focuses on adaptive optics. Astronomical optics. Atmospheric optics. Biomedical optics. Cardinal points. Collimation. Doppler effect. Fiber optics. Fourier optics. Geometrical optics (Gradient index optics. Holography. Infrared optics. Integrated optics. Laser applications. Laser optical systems. Lasers. Light amplification. Light diffraction. Luminescence. Microoptics. Nano optics. Ocean optics. Optical computing. Optical devices. Optical imaging. Optical materials. Optical metrology. Optical microscopy. Optical properties. Optical signal processing. Optical testing techniques. Optical wave propagation. Paraxial optics. Photoabsorption. Photoexcitations. Physical optics. Physiological optics. Quantum optics. Segmented optics. Spectra. Statistical optics. Surface optics. Ultrafast optics. Wave optics. X-ray optics.

name: Plasma Physics
description: Plasma physics focuses on fundamental plasma physics. Magnetically Confined Plasmas (includes magnetic fusion energy research). High Energy Density Plasmas (inertial confinement plasmas, laser-plasma interactions). Ionospheric, Heliophysical, and Astrophysical plasmas (includes sun and solar system plasmas). Lasers, Accelerators, and Radiation Generation. Low temperature plasmas and plasma applications (include dusty plasmas, semiconductor etching, plasma-based nanotechnology, medical applications). Plasma Diagnostics, Engineering and Enabling Technologies (includes fusion reactor design, heating systems, diagnostics, experimental techniques).

name: Popular Physics
description: Popular physics focuses on explaining complex physics concepts in a way that is understandable without requiring advanced knowledge of the field. It may cover a wide range of subjects within physics and often aim to inform, educate, or inspire interest in physics among non-specialists.

name: Physics and Society
description: Physics and society focuses on structure, dynamics and collective behavior of societies and groups (human or otherwise). Quantitative analysis of social networks and other complex networks. Physics and engineering of infrastructure and systems of broad societal impact (e.g., energy grids, transportation networks).

name: Space Physics
description: Space physics focuses on space plasma physics. Heliophysics. Space weather. Planetary magnetospheres, ionospheres and magnetotail. Auroras. Interplanetary space. Cosmic rays. Synchrotron radiation. Radio astronomy.

name: Quantum Physics
description: Quantum physics focuses on fundamental aspects of quantum mechanics, including quantum entanglement, quantum coherence, quantum cryptography, quantum computation, quantum information theory, and various other topics related to the quantum nature of physical systems.

name: Biomolecules
description: Biomolecules in quantitative biology focuses on DNA, RNA, proteins, lipids, etc.; molecular structures and folding kinetics; molecular interactions; single-molecule manipulation.

name: Cell Behavior
description: Cell behavior in quantitative biology focuses on cell-cell signaling and interaction; morphogenesis and development; apoptosis; bacterial conjugation; viral-host interaction; immunology.

name: Genomics
description: Genomics in quantitative biology focuses on DNA sequencing and assembly; gene and motif finding; RNA editing and alternative splicing; genomic structure and processes (replication, transcription, methylation, etc); mutational processes.

name: Molecular Networks
description: Molecular networks in quantitative biology focuses on gene regulation, signal transduction, proteomics, metabolomics, gene and enzymatic networks.

name: Neurons and Cognition
description: Neurons and cognition in quantitative biology focuses on synapse, cortex, neuronal dynamics, neural network, sensorimotor control, behavior, and attention.

name: Other Quantitative Biology
description: Other quantitative biology interdisciplinary and quantitative biological studies that do not neatly fit into a specific category. It applies quantitative methods to biological problems and may include novel computational techniques, theoretical biology, mathematical models, and other innovative approaches to understanding biological systems.

name: Populations and Evolution
description: Populations and evolution in quantitative biology focuses on population dynamics, spatio-temporal and epidemiological models, dynamic speciation, co-evolution, biodiversity, foodwebs, aging; molecular evolution and phylogeny; directed evolution; origin of life.

name: Quantitative Methods
description: Quantitative methods in quantitative biology focuses on all experimental, numerical, statistical and mathematical contributions of value to biology.

name: Subcellular Processes
description: Subcellular processes in quantitative biology focuses on assembly and control of subcellular structures (channels, organelles, cytoskeletons, capsules, etc.); molecular motors, transport, subcellular localization; mitosis and meiosis.

name: Tissues and Organs
description: Tissues and organs in quantitative biology focuses on blood flow in vessels, biomechanics of bones, electrical waves, endocrine system, and tumor growth.

name: Computational Finance
description: Computational finance in quantitative finance focuses on computational methods, including Monte Carlo, PDE, lattice and other numerical methods with applications to financial modeling.

name: Economics
description: Economics in quantitative finance focuses on general methodological, applied, and empirical contributions to economics, including micro and macro economics, international economics, theory of the firm, labor economics, and other economic topics outside finance.

name: General Finance
description: General finance in quantitative finance focuses on the development of general quantitative methodologies with applications in finance.

name: Mathematical Finance
description: Mathematical finance in quantitative finance focuses on mathematical and analytical methods of finance, including stochastic, probabilistic and functional analysis, algebraic, geometric and other methods.

name: Portfolio Management
description: Portfolio management in quantitative finance focuses on security selection and optimization, capital allocation, investment strategies and performance measurement.

name: Pricing of Securities
description: Pricing of securities in quantitative finance focuses on valuation and hedging of financial securities, their derivatives, and structured products.

name: Risk Management
description: Risk management in quantitative finance focuses on measurement and management of financial risks in trading, banking, insurance, corporate and other applications.

name: Statistical Finance
description: Statistical finance in quantitative finance focuses on statistical, econometric and econophysics analyses with applications to financial markets and economic data.

name: Trading and Market Microstructure
description: Trading and market microstructure in quantitative finance focuses on market microstructure, liquidity, exchange and auction design, automated trading, agent-based modeling and market-making.

name: Applications
description: Applications in statistics focuses on Biology, Education, Epidemiology, Engineering, Environmental Sciences, Medical, Physical Sciences, Quality Control, Social Sciences.

name: Computation
description: Computation in statistics focuses on Algorithms, Simulation, Visualization.

name: Methodology
description: Methodology in statistics focuses on Design, Surveys, Model Selection, Multiple Testing, Multivariate Methods, Signal and Image Processing, Time Series, Smoothing, Spatial Statistics, Survival Analysis, Nonparametric and Semiparametric Methods.

name: Machine Learning
description: Machine learning in statistics focuses on machine learning (supervised, unsupervised, semi-supervised learning, graphical models, reinforcement learning, bandits, high dimensional inference, etc.) with a statistical or theoretical grounding.

name: Other Statistics
description: Other statistics focuses on works that are either interdisciplinary, apply novel statistical methods in unique ways, or cover topics in statistics that are not adequately represented by the other, more specific categories.

name: Statistics Theory
description: Statistics theory focuses on applied, computational and theoretical statistics. In particular, this includes statistical inference, regression, time series, multivariate analysis, data analysis, Markov chain Monte Carlo, design of experiments, and case studies. It also includes Asymptotics, Bayesian Inference, Decision Theory, Estimation, Foundations, Inference, Testing.
